svg.text: Flow A — Corti Web UI · Step-by-Step
Paste a note into Corti console → MCP → pipeline → result rendered back in browser
A1
You paste the clinical note into Corti Agent browser (console.corti.ai)
Corti LLM reads message → recognises registered Custom Expert (your MCP server) → decides to call run_pipeline tool
A2
Corti backend sends HTTP POST to ngrok public URL
POST https://<ngrok-id>.ngrok-free.dev/mcp · Content-Type: application/json · MCP jsonrpc 2.0
ngrok decrypts TLS → forwards plain HTTP to localhost:3456 · your Express server receives tools/call request
A3
MCP Server validates and routes (src/mcpServer.ts)
Reads method=tools/call · name=run_pipeline · extracts clinicalNote from params.arguments → passes to fixMojibake()
A4
fixMojibake() — repair corrupted characters from browser encoding
Browser encoding corrupts: Â°C→°C Âµmol→µmol Ã—→× â€"→— â¹→⁹
Strategy: swap × and — to placeholders FIRST (byte overlap risk) → Buffer latin1→utf8 repair → restore placeholders
Why order matters: × bytes (0xC3 0x97) overlap with — detection byte (0x97). Wrong order destroys × permanently.
A5
Layer 1 — Regex PHI Detection (src/phiDetector.ts → regexSpans)
17 patterns run locally: SSN · phone (US formats) · email · dates (5 formats) · MRN · NPI · Patient name · family member names · address · facility · ZIP
Each match → span {value, start, end}. Spans trimmed for leading/trailing whitespace. Runs in milliseconds, no network.
A6
Layer 2 — NER Detection (nerSpans → compromise.js offline NLP)
doc.people() · doc.places() · doc.organizations() — all offline, no API calls
Filters: relationship words removed (son/wife/sister/carer etc.) · medical eponyms removed (glasgow/ottawa/wells/tokyo etc.)
Explicit Dr. pattern: /\bDr\.?\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z-]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z-]+)*/ ← hyphen in char class catches Mei-Xing Loh fully
A7
Component Expansion → Layer 3 Consistency Pass
expandWithComponents(): splits "Wei-Lin Huang" → Wei, Lin, Huang · "Kevin Huang" → Kevin, Huang · filters COMMON_WORDS (medical/center/university…)
consistencySpans(): full-text sweep for every known PHI value + components · word-boundary check · skips bare numerics ≤4 digits to prevent year false positives
A8
Span merge + Codename assignment + AES-256-GCM encryption
mergeOverlapping(): sort by start, longest wins on overlap · Replace right-to-left (preserves indices) · unique value → unique codename <<WORD-XXXX>>
AES-256-GCM: per-run 256-bit random key · phi_map.enc (ciphertext + auth tag) + patient_key.b64 stored separately
A9
Facts Extraction — anonymized text sent to Corti LLM (src/factsExtractor.ts)
ONLY STEP WHERE DATA LEAVES LOCAL MACHINE. Sends anonymized text with <<CODENAMES>>. LLM extracts diagnoses, vitals, meds, age, sex → facts.json
ICD-10 Classification + ZK Proof Generation (snarkjs Groth16)
getDiagnosisChapter(): diagnoses-first iteration → keyword match to ICD chapter. snarkjs.groth16.fullProve(): 279 constraints on BN128 curve
Private inputs: ageYears, ageBracketLow, diagnosisChapter, patientKeyFp, factsFp (field elements mod BN128 prime). Local verify → proof.json + public_signals.json
MCP response bundle returned → ngrok → Corti LLM → rendered in browser chat
JSON: anonymizedText · facts (diagnoses, vitals, meds) · zkSummary (ageRange, icdChapter, verified:true, system:groth16, constraints:279)
Corti formats tool response → displays de-identified note + structured facts + ZK proof panel in conversation UI
PHI never leaves local machine
Steps A4–A8 run entirely locally (regex + NLP + crypto) · Only <<CODENAMES>> in step A9 · ZK proof in A10 proves age+ICD without revealing values
